title: Adam Silver Supports Grizzlies Playing Select Games In Nashville While Backing Memphis Long-Term
summary: NBA Commissioner Adam Silver expressed confidence in the Memphis Grizzlies' long-term viability Wednesday while acknowledging he would welcome a greater franchise presence in Nashville, responding directly to recent remarks by LeBron James suggesting the team relocate. Speaking on Barstool's "Pardon My Take" podcast, Silver confirmed that Grizzlies governor Robert Pera has expressed no interest in leaving Memphis. Silver did, however, float the idea of the club playing occasional games in the state capital. "Nashville is a city on the rise," Silver said. "If it were up to me, I'd love for them to play a few games a year in Nashville and sort of be Tennessee's team to the extent they can." James sparked the discussion earlier in April during a "Bob Does Sports" appearance, stating that NBA players widely dislike visiting Memphis and that Nashville, home to the NHL's Predators and NFL's Titans, would be a more suitable destination. "Go over to Nashville," James said. "They got everything." Silver disputed James' characterization of player sentiment toward Memphis. "Players I talk to all the time like playing in Memphis," Silver said. "Memphis has been a great market, historically for the NBA, amazing history in that city, amazing culture." There is regional precedent for split home schedules. The San Antonio Spurs have played two regular-season games at Austin's Moody Center in each of the past four seasons as part of their I-35 Series. However, Memphis and Nashville are separated by 213 miles, compared to just 77 miles between San Antonio and Austin. The Grizzlies' FedExForum lease expires after the 2028-29 season. The facility opened in 2004, three years after the franchise relocated from Vancouver. "From my standpoint, there's no reason why the Memphis Grizzlies can't be successful," Silver said.

title: Raptors Parent Company Plans $18.25 Billion Sports Entity After MLSE Buyout
summary: Rogers Communications, majority owner of the Toronto Raptors through its 75% stake in Maple Leaf Sports & Entertainment, announced plans Wednesday to complete its full acquisition of MLSE and consolidate its sports holdings into a single entity valued at more than $18.25 billion. CEO Tony Staffieri confirmed on the company's first-quarter earnings call that Rogers expects to purchase the remaining 25% MLSE minority interest, held by Larry Tanenbaum's Kilmer Sports Ventures, in the second half of 2025. "Following the close, we plan to combine our assets into one of the most significant sports ownership, media and entertainment entities globally," Staffieri said. Rogers acquired BCE's 37.5% MLSE stake in 2025, bringing its ownership to 75%. MLSE controls the NBA's Raptors, NHL's Maple Leafs, and MLS's Toronto FC. Rogers separately owns MLB's Blue Jays. The company intends to bring in minority investors for the consolidated sports group, using proceeds to reduce debt. CFO Glenn Brandt said the recapitalization process is expected to conclude in late 2026 or early 2027. Current Sportico valuations place the Raptors at $4.66 billion, the Maple Leafs at $4.25 billion, the Blue Jays at $2.9 billion, and Toronto FC at $730 million, a combined $12.5 billion. The broader entity would also incorporate Rogers' Sportsnet media assets. Rogers stock surged nearly 14% Wednesday, closing at $37.48, following guidance of reduced capital spending and stronger free cash flow. First-quarter revenue and earnings largely met analyst expectations.

title: Aspiration Founder Cooperated With NBA's Kawhi Leonard Salary Cap Investigation
summary: Joseph Sanberg, the Aspiration co-founder facing federal sentencing April 27, provided substantial cooperation to the NBA's ongoing salary cap circumvention investigation involving Kawhi Leonard and the Los Angeles Clippers, the league's lead investigator confirmed in a court letter. David Anders of Wachtell Lipton stated in an April 17 filing that Sanberg sat for two in-person interviews, supplied relevant documents, and delivered information consistent with the league's broader review of evidence. "Mr. Sanberg's cooperation substantially assisted our investigation, including our ability to develop a more complete understanding of key events," Anders wrote. The NBA launched its probe in September 2025 following reports that Leonard's $28 million Aspiration sponsorship was structured to circumvent the salary cap. Clippers owner Steve Ballmer had invested $50 million in the company in 2021, with the club separately announcing a $300 million, 23-year sponsorship agreement. Ballmer made an additional $10 million investment in March 2023. Sanberg previously pleaded guilty to two counts of wire fraud, with prosecutors alleging he defrauded investors of $248 million. Each count carries a maximum 20-year sentence. His attorneys are seeking leniency, describing him as motivated by purpose rather than personal gain. In a separate court letter, Ballmer's attorney stated the Clippers owner lost his entire $60 million Aspiration investment after the company declared bankruptcy in 2025, and has suffered extensive reputational damage. The Clippers lost nearly all $300 million in sponsorship payments and more than $20 million held in escrow. Ballmer has additionally been named in a civil lawsuit by 11 former Aspiration investors. He has denied all allegations and sought dismissal of the case.

title: Keldon Johnson Wins 2025-26 NBA Sixth Man Of The Year Award
summary: San Antonio Spurs guard-forward Keldon Johnson has won the 2025-26 NBA Sixth Man of the Year Award , earning the John Havlicek Trophy after a standout season off the bench. Johnson averaged 13.2 points and 5.4 rebounds in 23.3 minutes per game, becoming the only player in the league to appear in all 82 games as a reserve. He shot 51.9% from the field, his most efficient mark since his rookie campaign in 2019-20. His 1,081 bench points set a San Antonio franchise record and ranked second league-wide. The Spurs finished with the NBA's second-best record at 62-20. A panel of 100 global media voters determined the award winner. Miami Heat forward Jamie Jaquez Jr. finished second with 34 first-place votes and 51 second-place votes. Tim Hardaway Jr. placed third, followed by Naz Reid and Ajay Mitchell. Johnson becomes the second Spur to claim the honor, joining Manu Ginobili, who won the award in 2007-08. To qualify for consideration, a player must have come off the bench in more games than he started during the regular season.

title: Steve Kerr Meeting With Media Agents Ahead Of Warriors' Decision
summary: Steve Kerr has been meeting with media agents as his Golden State Warriors contract approaches expiration, with broadcasting, front office work, and a return to coaching all remaining possibilities, sources told Front Office Sports. Kerr has not yet settled on a next step. A spokesperson for his coaching agency, Priority Sports, declined to comment. Sources indicated the media meetings have taken place over several years and centered on broader long-term projects. All three current NBA broadcast partners, ESPN, NBC, and Amazon, would pursue conversations with Kerr should he choose to re-enter the media space, per the report. Kerr brings rare credentials to any potential broadcasting role. He won nine NBA championships total, five as a player, including three with Michael Jordan's Chicago Bulls and two with the San Antonio Spurs, and four more as Golden State's head coach. Kerr has also already demonstrated elite ability behind the microphone. Kerr served as TNT's lead color analyst in two separate stints alongside play-by-play voice Marv Albert, from 2003-07 and again from 2010-14, with a term as Phoenix Suns general manager between the two runs. Those broadcasts showcased sharp tactical analysis, polished timing, and a sense of humor, qualities that would make him a sought-after addition to any NBA rights holder's talent roster. Kerr would reenter broadcasting with an even higher profile and direct familiarity with the modern game.

title: Darius Acuff Jr. Declares For 2026 NBA Draft
summary: Arkansas guard Darius Acuff Jr. announced Wednesday on ESPN's "NBA Today" that he is entering the 2026 NBA Draft. The 6-foot-3 point guard is currently ranked the sixth-best prospect available, per ESPN's Jeremy Woo. "With that being said, I'm going to be declaring for the 2026 NBA draft," Acuff said. Acuff had a record-setting freshman campaign with the Razorbacks, earning SEC Player of the Year honors and a spot on the Associated Press All-America first team. He averaged 23.5 points, 6.4 rebounds, and 3.1 assists while shooting 44% from three-point range, leading Arkansas to the Sweet 16. The announcement continues a notable pattern under coach John Calipari. Derrick Rose, John Wall, De'Aaron Fox, and Shai Gilgeous-Alexander are among the lottery picks who developed under Calipari at Memphis and Kentucky. Acuff cited that track record as a factor in his decision to sign with Arkansas. NBA Hall of Famer Allen Iverson also played a formative role in Acuff's development and style of play. The two have built a relationship in recent months. "We've got a lot of similarities. He's one of the reasons I got cornrows, so he inspired me a lot," Acuff said. "He always told me to just play every game like it's your last." Acuff said watching Tuesday's Los Angeles Lakers-Houston Rockets playoff game reinforced his ambitions at the next level. "I see myself there, for sure. That's one of my goals," he said.

title: Austin Reaves Closer To Returning Than Luka Doncic
summary: The Los Angeles Lakers have taken a 2-0 series lead over the Houston Rockets, despite being shorthanded. Now, Luka Doncic and Austin Reaves are starting the process of ramping up for a return. Reaves is closer to being able to play than Doncic. "The sense around the Lakers is that Austin Reaves is actually the one that's further along than Luka Doncic in their respective rehab processes," ESPN NBA insider Shams Charania said. "I am told Austin Reaves has started one-on-one on-court work. The next step for him is to continue to go through the progressions of three-on-three, five-on-five. "Remember, early April, he had a four-to-six-week timetable. So theoretically that puts him on track (to return) late in this series (or) potentially early in the next series." Doncic has started some light and limited on-court work. There's been no update for Doncic's timeframe to return, but the Lakers do not expect the superstar to return until the second round, should they advance that far.

title: Marcus Smart Dominates Kevin Durant, Lakers Take 2-0 Series Lead Over Rockets
summary: Marcus Smart delivered a standout two-way performance Tuesday, holding Kevin Durant in check while scoring 25 points to lead the Los Angeles Lakers to a 101-94 Game 2 victory over the Houston Rockets. Durant, returning from a right knee injury that sidelined him for Game 1, finished with 23 points. However, he shot just 1-for-3 and committed three of his playoff career-worst nine turnovers across 18 possessions guarded by Smart, per ESPN Research. Smart finished with 8-for-13 shooting, five 3-pointers, seven assists, and five steals. His fifth three-pointer, with 2:23 remaining, extended a five-point advantage to eight and effectively sealed the outcome. "Smart, he just had a killer game today," said Lakers coach JJ Redick. LeBron James paced Los Angeles with 28 points, eight rebounds, and seven assists. James praised Smart's composure, noting his value with Austin Reaves and Luka Doncic both sidelined. "He's not afraid of the moment. He's always been assigned some of the best players that [have] ever played this game in his career. So, to have someone like that it just brings a lot of composure to our team," James said. Redick confirmed Reaves has begun on-court workouts while recovering from a Grade 2 oblique strain. Doncic, sidelined with a Grade 2 left hamstring strain, has not yet started his return-to-play progression. Neither player has a set return date. Houston shot 40.4% and has scored below 100 points in consecutive games. The series shifts to Houston for Game 3 on Friday with the Rockets 9.5 point favorites . Te Rockets haven't won a playoff series since 2020 when they defeated the Oklahoma City Thunder in seven games in the first round. Smart, a 2021-22 Defensive Player of the Year who reached the 2022 NBA Finals with Boston, reflected on his resurgence following injury-hampered stints in Memphis and Washington. "I'm very grateful to be doing this," Smart said. "I thank God every day, because I could have been out the league, right?"

title: Scoot Henderson On Scoring 31 Points In Blazers' Game 2 Win: 'Attack, Get Downhill'
summary: Scoot Henderson scored 31 points as the Portland Trail Blazers defeated the San Antonio Spurs 106-103 to win Game 2 and even the series. title: VJ Edgecombe Has Historic Rookie Playoff Performance As Sixers Take Game 2 Over Celtics
summary: VJ Edgecombe delivered one of the most remarkable rookie playoff performances in NBA history Tuesday, scoring 30 points and grabbing 10 rebounds while battling a back injury as the Philadelphia 76ers defeated the Boston Celtics 111-97 to level their first-round series at 1-1. At 20 years and 265 days old, Edgecombe became the youngest player in NBA history to record 30 points and 10 rebounds in a playoff game, surpassing Magic Johnson, who was 20 years and 276 days old during his celebrated 42-point, 15-rebound showing in Game 6 of the 1980 NBA Finals. Edgecombe is also the first rookie to post those numbers in a playoff game since Tim Duncan's 33-point, 10-rebound effort in 1998. Tyrese Maxey added 29 points and nine assists for Philadelphia, which shot 47.8 percent from the field and connected on 19-of-39 three-point attempts. Edgecombe knocked down six of those triples. "I think we knew where the shots were going to come from," Edgecombe said. "We kept trusting each other. Everyone can make a play on the court. They wanted me to shoot the ball." The performance was made more striking by the circumstances. Edgecombe took a hard fall early and left for the locker room in visible discomfort twice, once in the first quarter and again in the third, before returning both times to continue scoring. Philadelphia closed as a 13.5-point underdog , making this the franchise's largest upset victory in a playoff game in 35 years. "This is who we are. Game 1 was not who we are," Maxey said. Jaylen Brown led all scorers with 36 points but acknowledged the 76ers' young star earned his performance. "I thought Edgecombe was just too comfortable," Brown said. "He's a rookie, but he can play. We've got to be better on him." The Celtics shot 39.3 percent overall and went 13-of-47 from three, committing 13 turnovers that produced 16 Philadelphia points. Jayson Tatum finished with 19 points, 14 rebounds, and nine assists.

title: Duke Lands No. 3 Transfer John Blackwell From Wisconsin
summary: John Blackwell, the third-ranked transfer in this cycle, has committed to Duke, head coach Jon Scheyer announced Tuesday. The former Wisconsin guard averaged 19.1 points, 5.1 rebounds, and 2.3 assists last season, earning All-Big Ten third-team recognition. The 6-foot-4 rising senior is the most significant transfer acquisition of Scheyer's tenure and is expected to slot directly into the starting lineup. Duke projects as a top-five program entering next season. Blackwell was the premier scoring guard available in the portal this spring. He connected on 38.9 percent of 7.3 three-point attempts per game, including 44.2 percent on catch-and-shoot opportunities. On January 10, he dropped 26 points with four three-pointers to hand eventual national champion Michigan its first defeat of the season. His arrival fits a deliberate organizational shift toward experience. Duke recently secured the returns of point guard Cayden Boozer and center Patrick Ngongba II, giving Scheyer multiple veteran pieces despite likely losing forward Cameron Boozer and wing Isaiah Evans to the NBA draft. Blackwell's postseason track record strengthens his case further. He posted 22 points and 10 rebounds in Wisconsin's first-round NCAA Tournament loss to High Point and averaged 26 points across three Big Ten tournament games. Beyond scoring, Blackwell brings reliability at the free-throw line, shooting 86.3 percent last season, and draws fouls at an elite rate, five per 40 minutes, ranking in the top 300 nationally per KenPom. Duke's incoming freshman class remains elite, featuring top-15 prospects Cam Williams, Deron Rippey Jr., and Bryson Howard. With multiple returning starters now joined by a seasoned transfer, the Blue Devils enter next season as a credible national championship contender.

title: Victor Wembanyama Suffers Concussion After Hitting Floor In Game 2 Loss
summary: Victor Wembanyama has been diagnosed with a concussion after he was hurt early in the second quarter of the San Antonio Spurs' 106-103 loss in Game 2 to the Portland Trail Blazers. Wembanyama was driving to the basket against Blazers guard Jrue Holiday when he was fouled, lost his footing and fell forward, striking his head on the floor. He remained down briefly, appeared disoriented while attempting to rise and was ultimately escorted to the locker room for evaluation. Spurs general manager Brian Wright accompanied him. The team ruled him out shortly after Wright returned to the court as he was placed in the concussion protocol. "It's more than a basketball game for us, so I was just trying to check on my brother," Stephon Castle told NBC's Zora Stephenson at halftime. Under NBA concussion protocol, any player showing concussion symptoms must be removed immediately and evaluated in a distraction-free environment. A physician must also review video of the incident before any return to play is permitted. Should Wembanyama receive a formal concussion diagnosis, league rules bar him from playing for a minimum of 48 hours. Game 3 is scheduled for Friday in Portland. Wembanyama posted 35 points in Sunday's Game 1 victory and was named the NBA's Defensive Player of the Year on Monday . Wembanyama sat out 18 regular-season games this year, including all three matchups against Portland. San Antonio went 12-6 without him in the lineup.

title: Steve Kerr Unlikely To Remain With Warriors Barring Change Of Heart
summary: Steve Kerr entered the 2025-26 NBA season knowing it would likely be his last with the Golden State Warriors. Following Friday's play-in tournament defeat to the Phoenix Suns, the expectation around the league is that his 12-year run as Warriors head coach is finished. Multiple league sources confirm Kerr arrived at training camp mentally prepared to close out his tenure. Kerr firmly rejected contract extension talks last fall, signaling his intentions early. "I'm very comfortable just going into this season with a year left," Kerr said last September. "I'm so aligned with (general manager Mike Dunleavy) and (CEO Joe Lacob). There's no reason for discussion or concern. This is kind of a point in our relationship where it's like, let's just see how it is at the end of the year." A decision is expected within days rather than weeks. Sources say money will not be the determining factor in his choice. "They could offer Steve $25 million a year," one league source said, "and I doubt that alone would make a difference." Golden State constructed its roster around a single-season push. That plan unraveled through a devastating injury wave as Jimmy Butler missed 44 games following a torn ACL, Stephen Curry sat out 39, and Al Horford missed 37. Kerr deployed 43 different starting lineups in search of stability. Two sources indicate that any reversal would require Kerr feeling genuine confidence in his standing as the franchise transitions toward rebuilding. Kerr went on the record with ESPN saying that he was 50-50 on whether to return . Kerr arrived in Golden State in 2014 and built the NBA's most influential dynasty, introducing the celebrated "Death Lineup" and later winning four championships. The current Warriors roster, depleted and aging, bears little resemblance to those dominant squads.

title: Projected NBA Lottery Pick Thomas Haugh Returns To Florida For Senior Season
summary: Thomas Haugh has decided to return to Florida for his senior season. Haugh was a projected lottery pick in this year's draft had he declared as the 13th ranked prospect. Haugh appeared in 34 games last season with the Gators, averaging 17.1 points, 6.2 rebounds and 2.1 assists. "Most guys in my position in the draft, it would be a no-brainer to go to the NBA," Haugh told ESPN. "It's not just the NIL. It's a chance to play with my boys. To play for coach [Todd] Golden. To go to the school I love to play for. It was definitely a tougher decision than last year, but it was best for my career and future." Haugh is the first projected lottery pick to return to school since Michigan State's Miles Bridges in 2017. Haugh earned third-team All-American honors and first-team All-SEC honors this past season. The idea of Haugh returning for his senior season didn't appear to have any momentum until Haugh's family, and agent Aaron Klevan of THE·TEAM, approached Golden and asked what a potential return would look like in terms of situation and compensation. "They really didn't need to sell much," Haugh said. "Coach Golden and the staff did a great job, not pressuring me. They're my guys. They're gonna text me and call me regardless. They didn't do much recruiting. I grew up a Florida fan. Tim Tebow. The back-to-back national championships. The 2014 team, I remember. They didn't really have to sell me." Haugh figures to make more in NIL money in college than in his pick range of 11-15. "The unique angle that we were able to drive home to Tommy's family and Aaron Klevan, this dude has real bottom-line NIL value," Golden said. "That's an area right now where elite college athletes have an advantage over mid-tier pros. Tommy Haugh's legitimate NIL value at Florida is 10-20x what his NIL value would be on an NBA team next year. Because of the brand awareness at Florida, he will have been here for four years, all of those things along with him returning, our supporters really appreciate the loyalty."

title: Tiago Splitter Remains Leading Candidate For Blazers' Head Coaching Job
summary: Portland Trail Blazers owner Tom Dundon has conducted an extensive coaching search while the team competes in the playoffs, with multiple sources confirming that interim head coach Tiago Splitter remains the front-runner for the permanent position despite widespread interviews with other candidates. Reports suggesting Dundon seeks to fill the role for $1 million to $1.5 million are false, according to team sources. The focus of the search has been identifying the strongest candidate, regardless of cost. "Of all the things reported on Tom, the one thing that is not true is the coaching thing, that he is trying to get someone for $1 million to 1.5 million," a team source told The Athletic. "It's just not true. He's talked to everybody, and of course, some coaches he talks to would be less expensive than others. The goal is to find the best person." Dundon has spoken with St. Louis University coach Josh Schertz, Iowa coach Ben McCollum, former NBA coach Michael Malone prior to his hiring at North Carolina, and former New York Knicks coach Tom Thibodeau, who is no longer under consideration. "Tiago is going to be the leading candidate," a team source said. "People ask: Why don't we just give Tiago the job? That would be the easy thing to do. But ownership wouldn't be doing the team or the fan base justice if other people weren't interviewed." The ongoing search has drawn criticism from league observers. Splitter was thrust into the interim role before the season's second game when Chauncey Billups was arrested by the FBI in a gambling probe. "The amount of disrespect (toward Splitter) that's going on is beyond description," a league source said. "It's like, every day a new name is coming up. It's the most vicious thing I've encountered in 30-plus years." No formal offer has been extended to Splitter as of yet.

title: Rudy Gobert Holds Nikola Jokic To 1-Of-8 Shooting As Wolves Even Series With Nuggets
summary: Rudy Gobert delivered a defensive masterclass against three-time MVP Nikola Jokic on Monday, limiting him to 1-of-8 shooting in direct matchups as the Minnesota Timberwolves defeated the Denver Nuggets 119-114 to level their first-round series at 1-1. The performance marked the worst shooting game of Jokic's playoff career against a single defender, with a minimum of six shot attempts. Minnesota generated 1.157 points per possession compared to Denver's 1.109. The result carried added significance as Gobert placed fourth in Defensive Player of the Year balloting earlier on Monday , finishing behind Chet Holmgren, Ausar Thompson, and unanimous winner Victor Wembanyama. "Not the first time I've gotten disrespected," Gobert said. "Probably not the last. If you want to disrespect greatness, take it for granted, whatever, soon they'll realize the impact." Gobert's impact was most visible in the fourth quarter. Anthony Edwards instructed Gobert to defend Jokic straight up entering the final period, abandoning double-teams. Gobert then forced three straight misses in the closing minutes. "I told him we ain't bringing no double team," Edwards said. "You gonna guard [Jokic] one-on-one. Stop fouling. Stop going for the reach-in. Because he's going to flop. They're going to call the foul. Play him straight up." Jokic finished with 24 points, 14 of which came during a third-quarter stretch when Gobert sat with four fouls. Edwards battled through a right knee problem to post a team-high 30 points in 40 minutes. Julius Randle added 24 points and Donte DiVincenzo contributed 16, including a late three-pointer that provided the decisive margin. Postgame, Jaden McDaniels offered pointed comments about Denver's defense. "Go at Jokic, Jamal, all the bad defenders," McDaniels said. "Tim Hardaway [Jr.], Cam Johnson. Aaron Gordon. Their whole team. They're all bad defenders." Game 3 shifts to Minnesota on Thursday with the Nuggets -1.5 point favorites .

title: Mitchell, Harden, Mobley Power Combine For 83 Points In Cavs' Game 2 Win Over Raptors
summary: The Cleveland Cavaliers' three-headed offensive attack proved too much for Toronto once again. Donovan Mitchell scored 30 points, James Harden added 28, and Evan Mobley contributed 25 as they defeated the Toronto Raptors 115-105 in Game 2 Monday night, taking a 2-0 first-round Eastern Conference series lead. The victory extended Cleveland's postseason winning streak against Toronto to 12 straight, tying an NBA record for consecutive playoff wins against a single opponent. The Cavs hold identical 12-game active streaks against both Detroit and Atlanta. Cleveland generated 1.190 points per possession versus the Raptors' 1.086. The Cavaliers never surrendered the lead throughout the contest. "Your superstars step up," Kenny Atkinson said. "This is a superstar game. Those three led us. They hit the big shots. That's why these guys ... that's why they're stars." Mitchell and Harden combined to score or assist on 78 of Cleveland's 115 points, 68% of the team's total output, per ESPN Research. Atkinson highlighted their floor communication as a critical factor since Harden's mid-season arrival. "They're in constant conversations about what's going on out there in the huddle," Atkinson said. "De facto leaders, right? They're vocal and they see what's going on on both sides of it. ... That's why it helps to have two smart guys like that." Scottie Barnes posted a playoff career-best 26 points for Toronto, while RJ Barrett added 22. Brandon Ingram struggled badly, managing just seven points on 3-for-15 shooting. Both Mitchell and Harden publicly credited reserve forward Dean Wade's defensive work on Ingram as decisive. "As much as y'all talk about us three, Dean Wade deserves a bunch of credit for tonight," Mitchell said. Toronto committed a season-worst 22 turnovers. Game 3 is Thursday in Toronto with the Cavaliers -3.5 point favorites on the road .

title: Jeff Peterson Won't Take Shortcuts To Push Hornets Forward
summary: Charlotte Hornets GM Jeff Peterson is proud of the progress his team made, but won't take shortcuts to push his team into the playoffs next season. The Hornets fell to the Orlando Magic in the Eastern Conference Play-In Tournament's game for the eighth seed. "We can't skip steps," Peterson said. Look, we've made a ton of progress this season internally, and I'm excited because I know that's going to continue to happen. But we'll continue to be strategic and when the opportunity presents itself, I know we'll be ready because of the flexibility that we have." Charlotte has two picks in the first round of the 2026 NBA Draft. After selecting four rotation players a year ago, the Hornets have another opportunity to deepen their roster this year. "I'm very excited about this draft," Peterson said. "It's if not the deepest, one of the deepest that I've ever been a part of. A lot of good players all over the draft, of course. But again, where we are from an asset standpoint, it's going to allow us to be flexible. So whether that means we bring two players in, consolidate, we'll have different options that we'll continue to explore and look at. "But I'm excited for whoever we bring in because I know that it's going to be someone who is a Hornet, and about what we're about and at some point will contribute to what we are building here."
